steps.title: The commitment at risk
steps.explanation: Every investigation starts from business exposure, not a technical object. Horizon LNG Partners has a $250,000 commitment showing critical risk.
steps.targetObjectLabel: Horizon LNG Partners ITEM-NR-CPP-1000 risk cell
steps.expectedUserAction: Type "Horizon" into Universe Search, or click the 'Horizon LNG Partn...' row under Top Commitment Risks on the Dashboard.
steps.expectedApplicationState: selectedObjectId=RB-CPP-HORIZON; leftPanelMode can remain dashboard or switch to passport
steps.founderNarration: This is a live $250,000 commitment to Horizon LNG Partners. It's flagged critical. Let's find out why.

steps.title: The commitment's biography
steps.explanation: Open Passport to see the full picture: what this commitment is, why it matters, and what's connected to it.
steps.expectedUserAction: Click the 'Passport' toggle in the left-panel control group.
steps.expectedApplicationState: leftPanelMode=passport; Passport renders Overview/Current Risk/Relationships/Recommendations/Evidence/Timeline/Source Records/Documents for RB-CPP-HORIZON
steps.founderNarration: This is the commitment's full operational passport — everything OpsConductor knows about it, in one place, not spread across five ERP screens.

steps.title: See it in the graph
steps.explanation: Switch to Universe to see the commitment's real position in the operational graph — not a mockup, the live merged graph.
steps.expectedUserAction: Click 'Universe' in the Investigate control group.
steps.expectedApplicationState: workspaceLens=universe; camera focuses on RB-CPP-HORIZON with related nodes fanned left (Focus Mode)
steps.founderNarration: Every line here is a real relationship — supplier, lot, inspection, work order, customer commitment. This isn't a diagram we drew. It's derived from the data.

steps.title: Root cause: the engineering change
steps.explanation: The commitment's risk traces to an engineering change on the CPP-1000 impeller, triggered by a supplier casting issue.
steps.targetObjectLabel: ECO-NR-GOU-099 — CPP-1000 impeller clearance tolerance update
steps.expectedUserAction: Search "ECO-NR-GOU-099", or click the Relationships-section row for it from the commitment's Passport.
steps.founderNarration: Engineering opened a change order against Apex Foundry Group's casting. That change order is the pivot point of this whole story — it's also where our second scenario, the engineering-change investigation, begins.

steps.title: The affected material lot
steps.explanation: The ECO placed a hold on a specific incoming casting lot.
steps.targetObjectLabel: Lot LOT-APX-C1088 (heat H-7726, Apex Foundry Group)
steps.expectedUserAction: Search "LOT-APX-C1088" or click the ECO's 'places_hold_on' relationship row.
steps.founderNarration: This is the actual physical lot the hold applies to — received August 2nd, tied to a specific supplier heat number.

steps.title: The nonconformance
steps.explanation: Receiving inspection found a dimensional nonconformance and localized porosity on this lot — the quality event that forced the recovery plan.
steps.expectedUserAction: Search "NCR-NR-GOU-301" or click through from the lot's Relationships.
steps.founderNarration: One nonconformance record, one meaning, used consistently everywhere it's referenced — dimensional issue plus porosity on this one lot.

steps.title: The disposition decision
steps.explanation: Material Review Board approved a controlled rework path rather than scrapping the lot.
steps.targetObjectLabel: MRB-NR-GOU-117 — Use-as-is with engineering-approved rework traveler
steps.expectedUserAction: Search "MRB-NR-GOU-117" or click through from the NCR's Relationships.
steps.founderNarration: Quality didn't scrap the lot — they authorized a certified repair path. That's the decision that keeps this commitment recoverable instead of lost.

steps.title: The recovery vendor
steps.explanation: A certified outside-operation purchase order sends the affected casting out for weld repair.
steps.founderNarration: This is the actual outside-operation PO — the repair vendor, the cost, the schedule commitment that has to hold for Horizon's date to hold.

steps.title: The finish line: FAT
steps.explanation: Everything converges on the Factory Acceptance Test at Grand Junction — the gate that determines whether Horizon's commitment ships on time.
steps.founderNarration: This is the gate. Everything we just walked through exists to protect this test date, and through it, the Horizon commitment.

steps.title: Watch it unfold over time
steps.explanation: Scrub the Time slider to see the recovery narrative reveal in the order it actually happened.
steps.expectedUserAction: Drag the Time slider from Baseline through t3.
steps.expectedApplicationState: timeSliceId advances 0→3; timeline tick row shows past/current/future; breadcrumb stepper advances Executive Signal → Shortage → Recommendation → Operational Relationships
steps.founderNarration: None of this was pre-scripted for today — this is the same data, replayed on its own timeline.
